content: We can reframe the ICM curiosity mechanism's intrinsic reward signal to prioritize exploration of state transitions that closely resemble previously failed trajectories, turning high-uncertainty failure regions that standard ICM avoids into the primary source of intrinsically motivated exploration. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where failed attempts directly drive more targeted curiosity-driven exploration to resolve the uncertainty that caused the original failure.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
trigger_topic: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
